{{Quote|We've done enough damage for one day. I think the Corellians now understand that the Empire still rules this system.|General Weir|<ref name="XW:RL2">[[X-Wing: Rogue Leader 2|''X-Wing: Rogue Leader'' 2]]</ref>}}
The '''Raid at Coronet''' took place two weeks after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].

==Background==
The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces under the command of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Weir/Legends|Weir]] that were stationed in the [[Corellian system/Legends|Corellian system]] found out that [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] had died at [[Endor/Legends|Endor]]. Weir decided that they had to attack the capital city of [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]] to show the populace that the Empire was still in control. By chance, [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] now under the command of [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], were stationed in [[Coronet City/Legends|Coronet City]] on a scouting mission. When the Imperials hit, the city was taken by complete surprise.<ref name="Rogue Leader 1" />

==The battle==
The first wave of attack was two [[Storm commando/Legends|Storm commandos]] with [[Missile launcher/Legends|missile launchers]] destroyed a [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] that the Rogues' were eating at, almost killing Luke and [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]]. Skywalker drew his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and quickly dispatched the two [[Commando/Legends|commandos]]. As soon as Luke killed the first wave of commandos, the second wave of Imperials hit.<ref name="Rogue Leader 1" /> This wave was much larger, with at least three [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-STs]] and dozens of [[Scout trooper/Legends|scout]] and [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. Weir was commanding his forces from behind, directing them were to hit next.<ref name="Rogue Leader 2" />

[[File:Weir.jpg|thumb|left|180px|Weir during the battle.]]
Many citizens were killed by the Imperials as they assaulted the city. Rogue Squadron took cover in the ruins of the former cantina, fighting off the Imperial advance. During the battle, [[Ten Numb/Legends|Ten Numb]] found one of the missile launchers the Storm Commandos dropped earlier and shot an advancing AT-ST, killing the occupants and sending the vehicle toppling over. Luke Skywalker was able to catch the Imperial war-machine using [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] before it fell on a group of civilians. [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]] and Ten were able to get the people out of the way, just as Luke lost his grip on the AT-ST.<ref name="Rogue Leader 2" />

At the rear of the Imperial line, one of Weir's officers spotted the Rebels and their Jedi leader and told the General about them. Weir concluded that the Rebel with the lightsaber must be Skywalker, so he ordered the remainder of his forces to kill the Jedi and his allies. The Imperials attacked the Rebel line en masse, pushing Rogue Squadron back. Wedge saw the officer leading the assault and captured him, pinning him down on the floor and pointing a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] to his head.<ref name="Rogue Leader 2" />

[[File:LukefightsCommandos.jpg|thumb|right|[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] fighting [[Storm commando/Legends|Storm commandos]].]]
Weir saw that he had lost a large number of troops and decided that they had proved their point; the Empire was still in control. Weir and his men fled, leaving a large part of Coronet in ruin. Ten saw the Imperial retreat and commandeered a [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]] from a dead scout trooper and followed them. Meanwhile, Wedge and Luke interrogated the captured officer to discover why the Imperials attacked Coronet. Ten followed Weir back to the Imperial landing site, about a [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometer]] west of the city. As he watched the Imperials load up, Weir sneaked up on him. The General knocked him unconscious with the butt of his [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]].<ref name="Rogue Leader 2" />

==Aftermath==
As this was going on, Luke, [[Wes Janson/Legends|Wes Janson]], and Wedge were following Ten's [[Homing beacon/Legends|homing beacon]] to the landing site, but didn't get there fast enough. The Imperials launched with their new prisoner, Ten. Luke ordered the Rogues to get into their [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] and to follow Weir back to his hideout. Rogue Squadron would go on to follow the Imperials back to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tralus/Legends|Tralus]], where Weir had his hideout.<ref name="Rogue Leader 2" />
